content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

July 26, 2002


Daniel S. Hentschke, General Counsel
San Diego County Water Authority
4677 Overland Avenue
San Diego, CA 92123-1233

Re:    Your Request for Advice
       Our File No. A-02-142

Dear Mr. Hentschke:

       This letter is in response to your request for advice on behalf of Bernie Rhinerson
regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

QUESTION

       Does Mr. Rhinerson have a conflict of interest in a decision to proceed with a
pipeline project by virtue of an economic interest in Sempra Energy International?

CONCLUSION

       Under the Commission's regulations, it is presumed that Sempra Energy
International, one of Mr. Rhinerson's economic interests, will experience a material
financial effect from the decision, absent proof to rebut this presumption. Therefore, Mr.
Rhinerson has a conflict of interest in the pipeline decision by virtue of his economic
interest in Sempra Energy International and may not participate in this decision.

FACTS

       Bernie Rhinerson, a member of the San Diego County Water Authority
("SDCWA"), is a partner with a 1/3 (33%) ownership interest in Southwest Strategies,
LLC ("Southwest"), a public affairs firm. He receives a salary and a share of the profits

_______
¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. A-02-142
Page No. 2

from this business entity. San Diego Gas & Electric Company ("SDG&E") is client of
Southwest. SDG&E, a public utility that distributes gas and electricity to retail customers
in San Diego County, is wholly owned by Sempra Energy ("SEMPRA").

SEMPRA also wholly owns Sempra Energy International ("SEI"), which is being
considered for participation in a pipeline project before the board of SDCWA. SEI has
been proposed as the energy partner by the private sector developer of the pipeline. SEI's
participation relates to an electrical generation component to be used in the pipeline
project. You inquire only about a conflict of interest based on whether Mr. Rhinerson
has an economic interest in SEI.

ANALYSIS

The primary purpose for the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Act is to ensure
that "[public officials, whether elected or appointed, [should] perform their duties in an
impartial manner, free from bias caused by their own financial interests or the financial
interests of persons who have supported them." (Section 81001(b).) In furtherance of
this goal, section 87100 of the Act prohibits a public official from making, participating
in making, or otherwise using his or her official position to influence a governmental
decision in which the official has a financial interest.

Determining whether a conflict of interest exists under section 87100 requires
analysis of the following questions outlined below.²

Step One: Is Mr. Rhinerson considered a "public official"?

As a member of the SDCWA, Bernie Rhinerson is a "member, officer, employee,
or consultant of a state or local government agency" and, therefore, is subject to the
conflict-of-interest provisions of the Act. (Section 82048; Regulation 18701(a).)

Step Two: Is Mr. Rhinerson making, participating in making, or influencing a
governmental decision?

A public official "makes a governmental decision" when the official, acting
within the authority of his or her office or position, votes on a matter, obligates or
commits his or her agency to any course of action, or enters into any contractual
agreement on behalf of his or her agency. (Section 87100; Regulation 18702.1.) A
public official "participates in making a governmental decision" when, acting within the
authority of his or her position and without significant substantive or intervening review,
the official negotiates, advises or makes recommendations to the decisionmaker
regarding the governmental decision. (Section 87100; Regulation 18702.2.) A public
official is attempting to use his or her official position to influence a decision before his
or her own agency if, for the purpose of influencing, the official contacts or appears

2 These questions are based on the Act's conflict-of-interest analysis provided at Regulation 18700(b).

File No. A-02-142
Page No. 3

before any member, officer, employee, or consultant of his or her agency. (Section
87100; Regulation 18702.3.)

As a member of board of SDCWA, Mr. Rhinerson will "make a governmental
decision" if he votes on the decision to proceed with the pipeline project. Additionally, if
he engages in any of the actions detailed above with regard to this decision, he will
"participate in making" or "influence" that decision.

Step Three: What are Mr. Rhinerson's economic interests — the possible sources of
a financial conflict of interest?

Section 87103 provides that a public official has a "financial interest" in a
governmental decision "if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a
material financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on the
official, a member of his or her immediate family," or on any of the official's economic
interests, described as follows:

•

A public official has an economic interest in a business entity in which he or she
has a direct or indirect investment³ of $2,000 or more (Section 87103(a);
Regulation 18703.1(a)); or in which he or she is a director, officer, partner,
trustee, employee, or holds any position of management (Section 87103(d);
Regulation 18703.1(b));

•

A public official has an economic interest in real property in which he or she has a
direct or indirect interest of $2,000 or more (Section 87103(b); Regulation
18703.2);

•

A public official has an economic interest in any source of income, including
promised income, which aggregates to $500 or more within 12 months prior to the
decision (Section 87103(c); Regulation 18703.3);

•

A public official has an economic interest in any source of gifts to him or her if
the gifts aggregate to $320 or more within 12 months prior to the decision
(Section 87103(e); Regulation 18703.4);

•

A public official has an economic interest in his or her personal finances,
including those of his or her immediate family -- this is the "personal financial
effects" rule (Section 87103; Regulation 18703.5).

³ An indirect investment or interest means any investment or interest owned by the spouse of an
official or by a member of the official's immediate family, by an agent on behalf of a public official, or by
a business entity or trust in which the official, the official's immediate family, or their agents own directly, indirectly, or beneficially a 10-percent interest or greater. (Section 87103.) "Immediate family" is defined
at Section 82029 as an official's spouse and dependent children.

File No. A-02-142
Page No. 4

Mr. Rhinerson has an economic interest in Southwest because he is partner in this
business entity. Additionally, he has an economic interest in Southwest because he has
an investment of $2,000 or more in this business. Finally, assuming he has received
income aggregating to $500 or more from Southwest, he has an economic interest in this
entity.

Income of an individual also includes a pro rata share of any income of any
business entity or trust in which the individual or spouse owns, directly, indirectly or
beneficially, a 10-percent interest or greater. (Section 82030.) Therefore, because
Mr. Rhinerson has a 33% interest in Southwest, he is attributed with 33% of the income
from each source of income to Southwest. As a result, he has an economic interest in
SDG&E as a source of income to him, assuming he has received income from SDG&E
which is attributed to him on a pro rata basis aggregating to $500 or more within the 12
months prior to the time of the decision on the pipeline.

An official will also have a financial interest in a decision within the meaning of
section 87100 if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material
financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on a business
entity which is a parent or subsidiary of, or is otherwise related to, a business entity in
which the official has one of the interests defined in section 87103(a), (c), or (d).
(Regulation 18703.1(c).) "A parent-subsidiary relationship exists when one corporation
directly or indirectly owns shares possessing more than 50 percent of the voting power of
another corporation." (Regulation 18703.1(d)(1).) Because SEMPRA owns SDG&E, a
parent-subsidiary relationship exists between SDG&E and SEMPRA. Therefore,
Mr. Rhinerson also has an economic interest in SEMPRA.

"Business entities, including corporations, partnerships, joint ventures and any
other organizations and enterprises operated for profit, which do not have a parent-
subsidiary relationship or otherwise related if...[a] controlling owner (50% or greater
interest as a shareholder or as a general partner) in one entity also is a controlling owner
in the other entity.'" (Regulation 18703.1(d)(2).) Because SDG&E and SEI are both
wholly owned by SEMPRA, they are "otherwise related business entities."
Consequently, Mr. Rhinerson has an economic interest in SEI.

Step Four: Are the economic interests of Mr. Rhinerson directly or indirectly
involved in the governmental decisions?

In answer to your specific question, we conduct a full analysis (Steps Four –
Eight) only with regard to SEI. If Mr. Rhinerson determines that he would like additional
guidance as to Southwest, he should seek further assistance.

A person, including a business entity or source of income, is directly involved in a
decision before an official's agency when that person, either directly or by an agent:

4 Regulation 18703.1 provides two other ways, which are not applicable here, in which business
entities can be otherwise related.

File No. A-02-142
Page No. 5

"(1) Initiates the proceeding in which the decision will be
made by filing an application, claim, appeal, or similar
request or;
(2) Is a named party in, or is the subject of, the
proceeding concerning the decision before the official or
the official's agency. A person is the subject of a
proceeding if a decision involves the issuance, renewal,
approval, denial or revocation of any license, permit, or
other entitlement to, or contract with, the subject person."
(Regulation 18704.1(a).)

Under the Commission's regulations, business entities and sources of income which are
not directly involved under the rules stated above are considered indirectly involved for
purposes of choosing the materiality standard. (Regulation 18704.1(b).)

You have stated that SEI has been proposed as the energy partner for the pipeline
project. Because the decision before the board involves a potential contract with SEI for
the electrical generation component, SEI is the subject of the proceeding and is
considered directly involved in this decision.

Steps Five and Six: Will the financial effect of the decision on SEI be material and
reasonably foreseeable?

If a business entity in which a public official has an economic interest is directly
involved in a governmental decision, regulation 18705.1(b) applies. This regulation
provides, in pertinent part:

"…the financial effects of a governmental decision on a
business entity which is directly involved in the
governmental decision is presumed to be material. This
presumption may be rebutted by proof that it is not
reasonably foreseeable that the governmental decision will
have any financial effect on the business entity."⁵
(Regulation 18705.1(b)(1).)

Based on these provisions, it is presumed that SEI, one of Mr. Rhinerson's economic
interests, will experience a material financial effect from the decision, absent proof to
rebut this presumption. As a result, it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will
have a material financial effect on SEI. Therefore, Mr. Rhinerson has a conflict of
interest in the pipeline decision by virtue of his economic interest in SEI and may not
participate in this decision.

_________________________

⁵ This rule is applicable unless the exception of subdivision (b)(2) of regulation 18705.1 applies.
Based on the facts you have provided, it does not appear that this exception applies to Mr. Rhinerson's
economic interest in SEI.

File No. A-02-142
Page No. 6

Steps Seven and Eight: "Public Generally" and "Legally Required Participation"
Exceptions

The facts you have presented do not suggest that the final steps of the conflict-of-
interest analysis, exceptions to the conflict-of-interest rules, are applicable to Mr.
Rhinerson's situation.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: [signature]
Natalie Bocanegra
Staff Counsel, Legal Division

interest in Sempra Energy Intemational and may not participate in this decision.

QUESTION: Does Mr. Rhinerson have a conflict of interest in a decision to proceed with a
pipeline project by virtue of an economic interest in Sempra Energy International?

CONCLUSION: Under the Commission's regulations, it is presumed that Sernpra Energy
International, one of Mr. Rhinerson's economic interests, will experience a material
financial effect from the decision, absent proof to rebut this presumption. Therefore, Mr.
Rhinerson has a conflict of interest in the pipeline decision by virtue of his economic
interest in Sempra Energy Intemational and may not participate in this decision.
